domanda: Switches
opzioneA: "Have one MAC address per physical port."
opzioneB: "Have one MAC address per physical port plus one for each configured VLAN."
opzioneC: "Have a single MAC address for the whole device."
opzioneD: "Do not have any MAC address (they are transparent indeed)"

domanda: The Spanning Tree Protocol operates sequentially according to the following phases:
opzioneA: Root Bridge selection, Root Ports selection, Designated Ports selection.
opzioneB: Root Ports selection, Root Bridge selection, Designated Ports selection.
opzioneC: Root Bridge selection, Designated Ports selection, Root Ports selection.
opzioneD: Root Ports selection, Designated Ports selection, Root Bridge selection.

domanda: The (classical) Spanning Tree Protocol can have two types of frames:
opzioneA: Topology Change Notification BPDU and Topology Advertisement BPDU.
opzioneB: Configuration BPDU and Topology Change Notification BPDU.
opzioneC: Configuration BPDU and Topology Advertisement BPDU.
opzioneD: Root Bridge Election BPDU and Topology Advertisement BPDU.

domanda: The Spanning Tree Protocol is needed because:
opzioneA: The learning process in bridges cannot operate when the L2 network contains loops.
opzioneB: In case the network contains multiple paths, the load balancing algorithm can generate frame reordering problems in the destination host.
opzioneC: The learning process in bridges is not optimized in presence of multiple paths toward the same destination.
opzioneD: Frames can circulate forever since the Time-To-Live field in the Ethernet frame is not decremented by bridges.

domanda: According to the IEEE 802.1q (VLAN) standard, the GVRP protocol (GARP VLAN Registration Protocol) is useful to:
opzioneA: Register the traffic of different VLANs.
opzioneB: Assign different priorities to packets belonging to different VLANs.
opzioneC: Configure dynamically the ports of a switch based on the configuration obtained from the network interface of the computer at the other end of the cable.
opzioneD: Propagate automatically over the whole network the information about existing VLANs.

domanda: One of the reasons that motivates the use of VLANs is:
opzioneA: Faster frame forwarding.
opzioneB: Reduction of broadcast traffic (better scalability).
opzioneC: Reduction of the collisions (better scalability).
opzioneD: Simplified management of the Spanning Tree Protocol.

domanda: A Trunk port
opzioneA: Transmits only tagged frames.
opzioneB: Transmits only untagged frames.
opzioneC: Transmits mostly tagged frames.
opzioneD: Transmits only BPDU frames.

domanda: An IEEE 802.11 station is said to operate at the basic rate when:
opzioneA: It uses the minimum transmission rate available in a BSS.
opzioneB: It uses a modulation that provides the minimum bit time.
opzioneC: It uses the shortest time interval between frames.
opzioneD: It sends the minimum number of frames per data packets

domanda: Which of the following factors does NOT affect the throughput of a WiFi connection operating according to the IEEE 802.11ac (WiFi 5) standard?
opzioneA: The presence of hidden terminals.
opzioneB: The presence of walls between the stations and the AP
opzioneC: The presence of other IEEE 802.11ac stations within radio range.
opzioneD: The presence of Bluetooth devices nearby.

domanda: With the term InterFrame Space, the IEEE 802.11 standard refers to:
opzioneA: The different timing in the transmission of back-to-back radio frames by a station.
opzioneB: The radio range that allows a station to receive data frames from an Access Point.
opzioneC: The safety distance from an AP to avoid dangerous radiation exposure.
opzioneD: The duration of a radio frame.

domanda: To determine the Root Bridge, the Spanning Tree analyzes the value of the following field present in the BPDU:
opzioneA: Root Path Cost.
opzioneB: Root Identifier.
opzioneC: Bridge Priority.
opzioneD: Port Identifier.

domanda: The Spanning Tree Protocol is used to:
opzioneA: Manage multiple paths in load-balancing, providing redundancy in the network.
opzioneB: Update the filtering database.
opzioneC: Optimize the forwarding process.
opzioneD: Transform a network containing meshes into a tree, removing loops.

domanda: Given two bridges connected to the same L2 network, in which the Bridge Identifiers are 32768-00:11:22:33:44:55 and 28672-00:22:33:44:55:66:
opzioneA: The value 28672 is not a valid Bridge Priority.
opzioneB: The first will become the root bridge.
opzioneC: The second will become the root bridge.
opzioneD: The second will become root bridge for the VLAN 0, while the first will become root bridge for all the other VLANs.

domanda: In a switched network:
opzioneA: The number of missing frames may not be negligible because of possible congestions on the switches.
opzioneB: Missing frames is a rare event thanks to the high speed of the switch.
opzioneC: There cannot be missing frames because of the Spanning Tree protocol.
opzioneD: There cannot be missing frames because there are no collisions.

domanda: When a Broadcast Storm occurs within a VLAN:
opzioneA: The broadcast storm occurs also in the other VLANs.
opzioneB: The broadcast storm occurs also in the other VLANs, unless a priority mechanism is used to privilege the traffic of those VLANs.
opzioneC: The other VLANs may suffer because of the bandwidth scarcity due to the large amount of traffic on the trunk links.
opzioneD: The other VLANs are protected and are not affected by this problem.

domanda: Two computers belonging to two different VLANs:
opzioneA: Both computers must use a network interface (NIC) with support for VLAN IEEE 802.1q tags.
opzioneB: Can never communicate by sending Ethernet frames directly to each other.
opzioneC: One of the two computers must use a network interface (NIC) with support for VLAN IEEE 802.1q tags.
opzioneD: Can communicate by sending Ethernet frames directly to each other.

domanda: According to the IEEE 802.1q (VLAN) standard, the link connecting two switches:
opzioneA: It can be Access only if the corresponding ports on the two switches belong to the same VLAN.
opzioneB: It can be either Access or Trunk.
opzioneC: It can be only Trunk.
opzioneD: It can never be Trunk.

domanda: In WiFi networks, why the 5Ghz band is preferable to the 2.4GHz band?
opzioneA: The 5GHz band allows a wider coverage for the WiFi cell.
opzioneB: The 2.4GHz band does not support rate adaptation.
opzioneC: The 5Ghz band has more available channels.
opzioneD: The 2.4GHz band requires a license fee.

domanda: The purpose of “Block ACKs” in IEEE 802.11e is:
opzioneA: Sending multiple ACKs to different transmitters.
opzioneB: Stopping the transmission of ACKs when the channel is congested.
opzioneC: Acknowledging the reception of multiple frames using a single ACK.
opzioneD: Sending multimedia data that does not need acknowledgements.

domanda: In an Inter-Controller Roaming procedure at Layer 2, the involved APs of each neighboring WiFi cell:
opzioneA: Offer the same SSID and IP subnet.
opzioneB: Offer different SSID and IP subnets.
opzioneC: Offer different SSID but the same IP subnet.
opzioneD: Offer the same SSID but different IP subnets.

domanda: The Spanning Tree Protocol operates sequentially according to the following phases:
opzioneA: Root Bridge selection, Designated Ports selection, Root Ports selection.
opzioneB: Root Ports selection, Designated Ports selection, Root Bridge selection.
opzioneC: Root Ports selection, Root Bridge selection, Designated Ports selection.
opzioneD: Root Bridge selection, Root Ports selection, Designated Ports selection.

domanda: The (classical) Spanning Tree Protocol can have two types of frames:
opzioneA: Root Bridge Election BPDU and Topology Advertisement BPDU.
opzioneB: Configuration BPDU and Topology Advertisement BPDU.
opzioneC: Topology Change Notification BPDU and Topology Advertisement BPDU.
opzioneD: Configuration BPDU and Topology Change Notification BPDU.

domanda: The Spanning Tree Protocol is needed because:
opzioneA: Frames can circulate forever since the Time-To-Live field in the Ethernet frame is not decremented by bridges.
opzioneB: In case the network contains multiple paths, the load balancing algorithm can generate frame reordering problems in the destination host.
opzioneC:
The learning process in bridges is not optimized in presence of multiple paths toward the same
 desination.
opzioneD: The learning process in bridges cannot operate when the L2 network contains loops.

domanda: One of the reasons that motivates the use of VLANs is:
opzioneA: Reduction of the collisions (better scalability).
opzioneB: Faster frame forwarding.
opzioneC: Reduction of broadcast traffic (better scalability).
opzioneD: Simplified management of the Spanning Tree Protocol.

domanda: Which method would you choose to assign a given VLAN to each end-user?
opzioneA: 802.1x-based assignment.
opzioneB: Cooperative assignment.
opzioneC: Port-based VLAN.
opzioneD: Transparent assignment.

domanda: A Trunk port:
opzioneA: Transmits only tagged frames.
opzioneB: Transmits only untagged frames.
opzioneC: Transmits mostly tagged frames.
opzioneD: Transmits only BPDU frames.

domanda: In HSRP, what happens when the active router fails?
opzioneA: All hosts lose internet connectivity for a few seconds.
opzioneB: All routers start and election process to define who is the new Active router.
opzioneC: Hosts automatically reconfigure their default gateway.
opzioneD: A backup router becomes active and takes responsibility of the virtual IP/MAC.

domanda: What is the main goal of HSRP (Hot Standby Router Protocol)?
opzioneA: To dynamically assign IP addresses to hosts.
opzioneB: To provide redundancy for the default gateway in a LAN.
opzioneC: To enable Layer 3 routing between hosts and the Internet.
opzioneD: To enable Layer 3 routing between VLANs.

domanda: In IEEE 802.11 networks, why is the basic rate used to transmit the PLCP header and preamble of each frame?
opzioneA: To match the transmission rate of the highest-speed client.
opzioneB: To match the transmission rate of the closest WiFi station.
opzioneC: To increase the channel utilization of the WiFi cell.
opzioneD: To ensure that all stations can decode critical control information.

domanda: What is the purpose of the DIFS (Distributed Interframe Space) in the 802.11 DCF MAC protocol?
opzioneA: It separates consecutive transmissions from the same sender
opzioneB: It is the interval a station waits before attempting access to an idle channel.
opzioneC: It is the time interval used by the AP to gain priority access to the medium.
opzioneD: It ensures that the receiver has time to decode the incoming data.
